intent.objective: Govern the full lifecycle of states and solutions, from pre-generation filtering to admissibility, consolidation, selection, validation, and total elimination.
intent.problemStatement: Traditional systems assume all possibilities are admissible before evaluation, leading to combinatorial explosion, unstable search, and weak convergence.
intent.designRationale: Replace uncontrolled possibility with a layered governance stack in which only states passing successive operators are allowed to persist.
intent.publicSummary: OGL is a governance stack that controls the right of states and solutions to exist, converge, stabilize, or be eliminated.

stackRoles.P_in: consolidate viable states toward a core
stackRoles.Bayes: optional soft ranking within the core
stackRoles.TuyCut: remove incorrect solutions and select valid ones
stackRoles.Brauer0: eliminate the whole structure when continuation is no longer justified

classification.paradigmShift: Shift from solving over all possibilities to governing which possibilities are allowed to exist at all.

stageFormulae.PreObsolete: PreObsolete(X) = { x in X | x is not obsolete }

stateTransitionMeaning: Stage 1 filters states before meaningful generation.
Stage 2 removes states that cannot converge.
Stage 3 pulls viable states inward toward a coherent core.
Stage 4 optionally ranks viable candidates probabilistically.
Stage 5 performs decisive elimination of invalid solutions.
Stage 6 validates final stability under the fixed-point law.
Stage 7 can terminate the entire structure if the game itself should not persist.
mathematics.existenceCriterion: A state is admitted to stable existence only if it survives governance and satisfies fixed-point consistency under U.
mathematics.eliminationCriterion: A system or state can be fully eliminated when continuation is structurally unjustified, degenerate, or no longer meaningful under governance.

axioms: Not every generated state deserves persistence.
Stable existence is defined by invariance under the governing law.
A governance layer may terminate the entire structure if continuation is no longer justified.
propositions: PreObsolete reduces low-value state generation before deeper compute begins.
ECO removes states outside the attraction basin of stable existence.
P_in increases coherence and reduces dispersion among viable states.
Bayesian ranking remains optional and is restricted to the governed core.
Tuy's Cut transforms viability into decisive correctness.
U validates whether the remaining state is truly stable.
Brauer Height = 0 acts as a final elimination operator over the whole game.
theoremSketches: Governed convergence sketch: if entropy decreases, coherence increases, invalid states are cut, and fixed-point error shrinks, the stack tends toward a stable regime.
Selective existence sketch: a state that does not pass admissibility, selection, and validation cannot claim stable existence.
Terminal elimination sketch: if structural continuation collapses to irrelevance, Brauer0 can lawfully eliminate the whole hierarchy.
semantics.convergenceStatement: OGL moves a system toward stability by filtering admissibility, consolidating viability, selecting correctness, and validating fixed-point consistency.
semantics.governanceStatement: OGL does not merely solve problems; it governs the right of states and solutions to exist.

pipeline.name: Pre-Obsolete Operator
pipeline.description: Blocks outdated, weak, or structurally irrelevant states before deep processing.

pipeline.name: Entropy Collapse Operator
pipeline.description: Filters states that cannot converge toward the attraction basin of the fixed-point law.

pipeline.name: Inward Consolidation Operator
pipeline.description: Pulls viable states inward toward a denser, more coherent core region.

pipeline.name: Bayesian Ranking (Optional)
pipeline.description: Optionally ranks candidates within the viable core using probabilistic updating.

pipeline.name: Tuy's Cut Operator
pipeline.description: Cuts away invalid solutions and retains the valid or decisive region.

pipeline.name: Fixed-Point Law U(x)=x
pipeline.description: Validates whether the selected state is stable under fixed-point invariance.

pipeline.name: Brauer Height = 0 Operator
pipeline.description: Eliminates the entire hierarchical structure when the game itself should be erased.

oiMapping.role: Controls whether weak or obsolete states are allowed to emerge.
Determines whether a state is allowed to continue existing.
Forms a coherent core from viable states.
Optionally orders viable candidates without defining existence itself.
Converts viable possibilities into decisive correctness.
Erases the whole hierarchy when continuation is no longer justified.

aiMapping.aiRole: A full governance architecture for controlling possibility, convergence, correctness, stability, and termination.
aiMapping.effectOnHypotheses: Filters weak hypotheses, consolidates viable cores, optionally ranks them, decisively cuts invalid branches, and validates stable outputs.
aiMapping.effectOnSearch: Reduces combinatorial explosion by separating pre-generation blocking, admissibility filtering, consolidation, decisive selection, and final validation.
aiMapping.effectOnHallucination: Suppresses unsupported outputs by requiring admissibility, coherent consolidation, decisive validity, and fixed-point consistency.
aiMapping.effectOnLatency: Adds governance overhead but can reduce wasted deep-search cost and unstable termination.

isaMapping.executionView: A macro-level governance pipeline that expands into seven ordered control phases: generation blocking, admissibility filtering, inward consolidation, optional ranking, decisive selection, fixed-point validation, and total elimination.

benchmarkProfile.scenario: Compare unrestricted search, search with pruning, probabilistic-only ranking, and the full OGL pipeline across reasoning, planning, economics, and scientific modeling tasks.

metrics.name: Operational Entropy
metrics.meaning: Entropy of the active possibility space under governance.

metrics.name: Inwardness
metrics.meaning: Coherence and density of the viable core region.

metrics.name: Selection Purity
metrics.meaning: Fraction of selected states that satisfy validity constraints.

metrics.name: Fixed-Point Error
metrics.meaning: Distance from invariance under the governing law.

metrics.name: Stable Output Rate
metrics.meaning: Fraction of outputs that remain valid and stable after governance.

metrics.name: Total Runtime
metrics.meaning: Total cost to reach governed stable output or governed elimination.

expectedEffects: Lower entropy than unrestricted or probabilistic-only pipelines.
Higher selection purity than soft-ranking-only systems.
Lower fixed-point error at accepted termination.
Higher stable output rate under constrained reasoning.

safetyGuards.condition: pre-generation filter removes too many viable states
safetyGuards.action: relax obsolete threshold and emit diagnostic trace

safetyGuards.condition: admissible space collapses below minimum viable support
safetyGuards.action: fallback to bounded mode and reopen limited search
safetyGuards.reason: Prevent premature elimination of recoverable states.

safetyGuards.condition: P_in forms a dense core but validity confidence drops
safetyGuards.action: return to ECO or reduce inward contraction strength
safetyGuards.reason: Avoid converging around a misleading core.

safetyGuards.condition: Tuy's Cut selects states later rejected systematically by U
safetyGuards.action: tighten selection constraints and emit mismatch report
safetyGuards.reason: Maintain alignment between selection and validation.

safetyGuards.condition: U accepts a state but downstream instability persists
Brauer0 is triggered while recoverable structure still exists
safetyGuards.action: delay elimination and require justification certificate

deploymentNotes.compilerView: Compile OGL as a macro-governance layer that wraps and orders all downstream operator execution.
deploymentNotes.runtimeView: Track entropy, inwardness, selection purity, fixed-point error, stable output rate, and elimination triggers across the full governance pipeline.
deploymentNotes.chipView: Map OGL to staged control hardware with explicit trace-sharing between filtering, consolidation, selection, validation, and elimination blocks.
integrationNotes: Use PreObsolete to control early generation cost.
Use ECO as the admissibility gate before expensive inference.
Use P_in to form a coherent core before ranking or cutting.
Use Bayes only inside the governed core if probabilistic ranking remains useful.
Use Tuy's Cut for decisive elimination of invalid solutions.
Use U as the final law-level validator.
Reserve Brauer0 for terminal governance cases.
